beats.text: A student walks through a forest. Seven labels float in a column, waiting to be filled.
Zoom out — the forest itself is the ecosystem. Everything alive and nonliving, connected.
Pull in — the student is one organism. A single human body inside that ecosystem.
Zoom into the student's face. The eye appears — an organ, one piece of the body's machinery.
Inside the eye, the retina unfolds — a tissue, a sheet of coordinated cells.
One cell highlights: a photoreceptor. Inside it, a mitochondrion appears — an organelle.
Zoom to the mitochondrion. A single ATP molecule snaps into focus. That's the molecule level — the bottom of the chain.
